basic_details.job_title: APPSC Medical Specialist Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 117 Junior Specialist, Junior Dental Specialist Posts
basic_details.department: Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of Arunachal Pradesh
basic_details.short_information: The Arunachal Pradesh Public Service Commission (APPSC) Recruitment 2026 notification released for 117 posts of Junior Specialist, Junior Dental Specialist. Candidates with BDS, Master of Dental Surgery, MS/MD Can Apply Online. The Online application process for APPSC Recruitment 2026 has started on 17-04-2026 and will remain open until 18-05-2026. The candidate shall apply online through APPSC website, https://appsc.gov.in/.

application_fee.details: PwBD candidates are exempted from payment of application fee as per Section 19 of the Arunachal Pradesh Rights of Persons with Disabilities Rules, 2018.

age_limit.age_limit: Maximum 50 years as on 18 May 2026 (relaxable by 5 years for Govt. Servants)

qualification.details: A recognised medical qualification included in the First or Part-II of the Third Schedule (other than licentiate qualification) of the Indian Medical Council Act, 1956. Holders of Part-II qualifications must also fulfil the condition stipulated in sub-section (3) of section (13) of the Act. Post-Graduate Degree in the concerned specialty mentioned in Section 'A', 'B', or 'D' as the case may be in Schedule VI or equivalent of Arunachal Pradesh Health Service Rules, 2000. The candidate (including Foreign Medical Graduates/Post Graduates) must be registered with Arunachal Pradesh Medical Council (APMC). | Medical qualification included in Part-I or Part-III of the Schedule to the Dentist Act, 1948 as amended from time to time. Post-Graduate Degree in the concerned specialty mentioned in Section 'A', 'B', or 'D' as the case may be in Schedule-VI or equivalent of Arunachal Pradesh Health Service Rules, 2000. The candidate (including Foreign Medical Graduates/Post Graduates) must be registered with Arunachal Pradesh Dental Council (APDC). | One Time Relaxation (GoAP): The Government of Arunachal Pradesh has granted One Time Relaxation vide GoAP Notification No. DMETR-11012(17)/1/2024 dated 7th February 2026, waiving off the requirement of 3 years' experience after Post Graduate Degree and 5 years' experience after Post Graduate Diploma in the concerned Speciality. | Candidates must not exceed 50 years as on the last date of submission of online application form (18th May 2026), in accordance with One Time Relaxation vide GoAP Notification No. DMETR-11012(17)/1/2024 dated 7th February 2026. Age relaxation of 5 years is available for Government Servants. Age concession for PwBD candidates and regular Govt. servants of Government of Arunachal Pradesh will be as per Government rules in force.

selection_process: Selection will be made through Viva-Voce / Interview only in accordance with the Arunachal Pradesh Health Service Rules-2000 amended vide Notification No. HFW-1/89(Pt) dated 31/10/2003. The total marks of Interview shall be 100 marks.
Candidates must score a minimum of 50% of the total marks in the interview to qualify for selection (as per Rule XI
Sub-rule (e) of APPSC Manual for Conduct of Examination-2024). Relaxation for PwBD candidates

shall apply as per the Government notification. Candidates will be shortlisted for interview based on information provided in the online application form. Eligible candidates must produce all original certificates/documents/mark sheets at the time of interview. If a candidate is found ineligible during document verification
candidature shall be rejected. The date for interview of eligible candidates will be notified in due course.
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only at https://appsc.gov.in. One Time Registration (OTR) is mandatory before filling the application form.

1. Complete One Time Registration (OTR): Visit https://appsc.gov.in and mandatorily register for OTR before filling the online application form. Candidates who have already registered in OTR can directly log in with the same user ID and password.

2. Fill the Online Application Form: The online application link will be active from 17th April 2026 (1700 hrs) to 18th May 2026 (1700 hrs). Fill all details carefully and accurately. The application form once submitted within the last date shall be complete in all respects — no additional time will be provided for further changes.

3. Upload Required Documents: Upload scanned copies of all required documents (as listed in Point E of the notification) in the prescribed format and file size. Ensure all uploads are clear and self-attested.
4. Pay Application Fee: Pay the applicable fee — ₹150/- for APST candidates, ₹200/- for others. PwBD candidates are exempted. Fee payment is through the online mode on the APPSC portal.

5. Submit Application: Read the DISCLAIMER provided in the online application form carefully before final submission. If multiple applications are submitted, the one with the highest Registration ID (RID) will be accepted. Ensure the highest RID application is complete in all respects.

6. Govt. Service Applicants: Candidates in Government Service must upload a copy of intimation submitted to their respective Head of Department or an NOC issued by the department along with the application form.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in APPSC Medical Specialist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 117 vacancies have been notified 115 for Junior Specialist (Allopathy) and 2 for Junior Dental Specialist under APPSC Advertisement No. 3/2026 for the Health & Family Welfare Department, Government of Arunachal Pradesh.

faq.question: Q2. What are the online application dates for APPSC Medical Specialist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: The online application window opens on 17 April 2026 at 17:00 hrs and closes on 18 May 2026 at 17:00 hrs; the age limit cut‑off date is also 18 May 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for APPSC Junior Specialist and Junior Dental Specialist 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: The maximum age is 50 years as on 18 May 2026, with an additional relaxation of 5 years for Government servants and further relaxations for PwBD and regular Arunachal Pradesh Govt. employees as per rules.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for APPSC Junior Specialist (Allopathy) 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: Candidates must hold a recognised medical qualification included in the First or Part‑II of the Third Schedule of the IMC Act, 1956, plus a Post‑Graduate Degree in the concerned speciality as per Schedule VI of Arunachal Pradesh Health Service Rules, 2000, and must be registered with the Arunachal Pradesh Medical Council (APMC).

faq.question: Q5. What is the qualification required for APPSC Junior Dental Specialist 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: Candidates must have a medical qualification included in Part‑I or Part‑III of the Schedule to the Dentist Act, 1948, a Post‑Graduate Degree in the concerned dental speciality as per Schedule VI of AP Health Service Rules, 2000, and registration with the Arunachal Pradesh Dental Council (APDC).

faq.question: Q6. Is post‑PG experience mandatory for APPSC Medical Specialist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: No, the Government of Arunachal Pradesh has granted a one‑time relaxation waiving the requirement of 3 years’ experience after PG Degree and 5 years’ experience after PG Diploma in the concerned speciality for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q7. What is the pay scale for APPSC Junior Specialist and Junior Dental Specialist 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: The posts carry Pay Matrix Level‑11 with a pay scale of ₹67,700 to ₹2,08,700 per month as per Arunachal Pradesh pay rules.

faq.question: Q8. What is the application fee for APPSC Medical Specialist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: APST candidates must pay ₹150, other candidates (General/UR) ₹200, while PwBD candidates are fully exempted from fee payment under the Arunachal Pradesh Rights of Persons with Disabilities Rules, 2018.

faq.question: Q9. What is the selection process for APPSC Medical Specialist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: Selection will be done purely through a Viva‑Voce/Interview of 100 marks; candidates must secure at least 50% of the total marks to qualify, subject to applicable relaxations for PwBD, and only shortlisted applicants will be called for interview after scrutiny of online applications.

faq.question: Q10. How can candidates apply online for APPSC Medical Specialist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Ans: Candidates must first complete One Time Registration (OTR) on https://appsc.gov.in, then fill and submit the online application form between 17 April and 18 May 2026, upload all required certificates (degree, PG, APMC/APDC registration, identity, category, PwBD, experience, NOC for Govt. servants) and pay the applicable fee online; in case of multiple applications, only the one with the highest Registration ID will be accepted.
